qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default CORS configuration reflects arbitrary origins enabling cross-origin data exfiltration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns Starlette CORS origin reflection from allow_origins=["*"] with allow_credentials=True at glances_restful_api.py:292-299, while the advisory root cause is the default missing authentication path, unauthenticated router dependency, default 0.0.0.0 bind, and process-list command-line exposure.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only final qualifying finding targets permissive CORS middleware behavior, not Glances' default unauthenticated REST API initialization and router setup.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-wvxv-4j8q-4wjq describes Glances running with authentication disabled by default when started with glances -w, leaving REST API routes without an authentication dependency.

The advisory names glances/outputs/glances_restful_api.py lines 259-272 for _password/_jwt_handler set to None when no password is provided, lines 477-480 for APIRouter without Depends(authentication), and lines 98-99 for default binding to 0.0.0.0.
The final finding report lists code locations glances/outputs/glances_restful_api.py:292, :295, and :296 and describes CORSMiddleware configured with allow_origins=["*"] and allow_credentials=True.

The finding's reproduction verifies reflected Access-Control-Allow-Origin and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials headers, not unauthenticated access caused by the advisory's missing authentication guard.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so runtime tiering and PoC execution were not applicable.
